name: Ordinal Numbers — First, Second, Third
whatIsIt: Ordinal numbers tell us POSITION or ORDER in a line: first, second, third, fourth... Counting numbers (1, 2, 3) tell HOW MANY; ordinal numbers (1st, 2nd, 3rd) tell WHICH ONE — where something stands in a row or a race.
whyNeeded: Order is everywhere: who came first in a race, the third house on the street, the second floor, the fifth in a queue. Ordinal words let us describe exactly which one we mean, not just how many there are.
realLifeUses: Who came first, second, and third in a running race
The second floor of a building, or the third house on a lane
Your place in a queue at the shop
The fourth day of the week, or the first page of a book

vocabulary.meaning: A number that shows position: first (1st), second (2nd), third (3rd)...
A counting number that shows how many: one, two, three.
Where something stands in an order or line.
The one at the very start and the one at the very end.

story.title: The Village Fair Running Race
story.text: At the village fair race, five children line up at the finishing rope. Anu breaks the rope first! 'Anu is FIRST!' cries the announcer. Ravi is right behind — 'Ravi is SECOND.' Then third, fourth and fifth cross the line. 'Counting tells us there were 5 runners,' says the announcer, 'but ORDINALS tell us WHICH one — first, second, third. Position, not just how many!'
story.extractedProblem: Five runners finish a race. Anu breaks the tape at the front. What position is Anu?
story.answerInStory: Anu is first (1st) — she is at the front of the finishing order.

visual.caption: Five in a row: the 1st is at the front, the 5th is at the end. Ordinals name each position.

teachingGallery.title: Which place in line? First, second, third…
teachingGallery.note: Start counting from the front of the line.

standardMethod.summary: Start counting positions from the front: the front one is first, the next second, and so on to the last.
steps: Find the START of the line or race (the front).
Count along: second, third, fourth... one position at a time.
The one at the very end is LAST.

alternateMethods.name: Point-and-say
steps: Point at the first one and say 'first'.
Stop at the one you want — that's its position.
alternateMethods.example: Point along a queue: first, second, THIRD — the third person.

workedExamples.problem: In a line of animals: cat, dog, cow, hen — what position is the cow?
steps: Start at the front: cat is first.

workedExamples.problem: Who wins a race — the one who comes FIRST or SECOND?
steps: First means the front, ahead of everyone.
The winner is at the very front.
The one who comes FIRST wins.

commonMistakes.mistake: Answering 'how many' when asked 'which position' (saying 5 for the position of the last of five).
commonMistakes.fix: 'How many' wants a count (5). 'Which position' wants an ordinal (fifth). The last of five is in FIFTH position.

commonMistakes.mistake: Counting positions from the back instead of the front.
commonMistakes.fix: Ordinals count from the FRONT (the start). The first is at the front, not the back. Find where the line begins and count from there.

commonMistakes.mistake: Calling the second one 'first' by starting the count at zero.
commonMistakes.fix: The very first one is 'first' (1st), not 'zero'. Start counting positions at ONE, from the front.

easy.q: In a queue, who is at the very front?
easy.explain: The front of the queue is first.

easy.q: A, B, C in a row — what position is B?

medium.q: Runners finish: Anu, Ravi, Sana. What position is Sana?
In a line of 5, which position is the LAST one?
hintLadder: Count from the front to the end.
medium.explain: The last of five is fifth.

challenge.q: Houses 1st to 6th on a lane. What position is the house right after the 3rd?
You are 2nd in a queue and one person leaves the front. What is your position now?

questions.q: Front of the line is the ___ position.
A, B, C, D — position of C?
In a row of 4, the last one is in which position?

teachBackPrompt: Line up some toys and race them! Teach someone at home to say which is FIRST, SECOND, THIRD... and explain the difference between 'how many' (counting) and 'which position' (ordinals).

revisionCard.summary: Ordinals show POSITION: first, second, third... Count from the FRONT, starting at first (not zero). 'How many' = counting; 'which one' = ordinal.

gameMission.title: The Sports Day Race
gameMission.brief: Line up the racers and call out who finished first, second, third — position, not just count.

realLifeProject: Watch or run a little race at home with family. Call out who is first, second and third — and which floor or house-number positions you can spot outside.
